recommendation.description: The recommendation, made explicitly conditional on the cruxes. If exploratory, lead with what's missing to decide, then a clearly-marked best guess.

crux.description: The unstated assumption Z behind the question, and the check that tells which frame is right (the user/analysis verifies it later).

reframe.description: null unless the presupposed approach is probably the WRONG decision/lever (not merely that an alternative exists), backed by a checkable crux. High bar — default null for a well-posed question.

cruxes.description: The assumptions the recommendation rests on, ranked by decision-sensitivity.

load_bearing.description: true if flipping this flips the recommendation.

verified.description: true ONLY if a grounded, re-openable evidence entry establishes it.

note.description: If conceded, how the recommendation changed; if rebutted, why it doesn't hold. Same-model self-critique is weaker than an independent adversary.

epistemic_grade.description: Single-model: default exploratory unless the load-bearing cruxes are verified by grounded evidence.

grounded.description: Count of evidence entries with re-openable provenance.

locator.description: Re-openable provenance, or empty string if none.

grounded.description: true ONLY if locator is a real, re-openable path/URL/quote. theory/prior_knowledge are never grounded.
